checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill = 13,794,000 (2013-11-02) declined to 6,022,000 (2014-02-01); IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill = 245,978,000 (2013-11-02) declined to 211,485,000 (2014-02-01)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 cash flow statement / income statement
10-K FY2014 cash flow statement / income statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?
Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss = -28,247,000 (2013-11-03 to 2014-02-01, Q4 FY2013) and -42,897,000 (2014-11-02 to 2015-01-31, Q4 FY2014), both markedly larger swings than corresponding revenue changes

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent = 27,752,000 (2012-01-28) declined to 25,843,000 (2013-02-02) even as gross accounts receivable rose from 173,315,000 to 200,327,000 (net AR plus allowance)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: No NT 10-K/NT 10-Q or 10-K/A filings appear in the chronology for the 2011-2015 period covered by the financial series; the last NT 10-K was filed 2008-04-16
Multiple DFAN14A, PREC14A, and SC 13D/A filings cluster around May 2015 (filing dates 2015-05-08, 2015-05-15, 2015-05-21, 2015-05-26)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, Goodwill and IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill span fiscal years 2009 through Q1 FY2015 (period end 2015-05-02)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-reserving for doubtful accounts as gross receivables grew, allowing accounts receivable, net and reported income to be modestly overstated during FY2012, with the allowance ratio subsequently normalizing

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent = 27,752,000 (2012-01-28) vs 25,843,000 (2013-02-02) while gross receivables rose from 173,315,000 to 200,327,000

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Inventory build-up in FY2013 amid declining revenue suggests possible delayed recognition of markdowns/obsolescence reserves, temporarily overstating inventory and gross margin before the subsequent inventory reduction

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet = 206,602,000 (2014-02-01) vs 183,127,000 (2013-02-02), +12.9%, while SalesRevenueNet declined 6.4% over the same period

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Concentration of large impairment-like write-downs and losses in Q4 of both FY2013 and FY2014 (a 'big-bath' pattern) suggests possible delayed recognition of asset impairment until year-end rather than as declines in performance emerged during the year

top_signals: Persistent, large divergence between net income and operating cash flow in FY2013 and FY2014 (net losses of -22.8M and -37.2M vs positive operating cash flow of 0.2M and 55.1M), driven by non-cash charges concentrated in Q4 of each year
Accounts receivable grew ~20% in FY2012 while revenue declined ~1.4%, coinciding with a declining allowance-for-doubtful-accounts ratio (from ~16% to ~13% of gross receivables)

Inventory grew ~13% in FY2013 while revenue fell ~6.4%, followed by an inventory correction the next year, consistent with delayed markdown recognition amid a three-year revenue decline (942.5M to 882.6M to 858.2M)
